Name: Current Events
Description: These 2 songs are the ones lighting up your recently played list

Name: US Holidays
Description: Although traditionally played during the pagan winter solstice season, these two songs deserve your consideration year round.

Name: Science
Description: These two songs pay tribute to the ancient Greek philosophers that posit all of the universe is created from the four basic elements of Fire, Water, Earth and Air.

Name: Potent Potables
Description: Melancholic music of African American folk origin, typically in a 12 bar sequence, these 2 songs exemplify this genre

Name: The Pen Is Mightier
Description: Paying homage to the infamously pompous playwright, Edward George Bulwer-Lytton, these two songs demonstrate that a songs lyrics can overshadow it's instrumentation

Name: Sports
Description: From CWC to NWA to WWWF to WWF to WWFE and finally landing on WWE, these two songs are actually not about acronyms but would benefit the organization for two original characters and their entrance themes

Name: Potpouri
Description: Reminiscent of the vague, flowery, allergy inducing smells of your childhood, these two songs could have played through the 8 track stereo (victrola) on the night of your conception

Name: Space
Description: The expanse that exists beyond Earth and its atmosphere, a near perfect vacuum containing a low density of particles, these two songs pay homage to the great unknown

Name: Food & Drink
Description: In a second nod to four elements, Samin Nosrat proposes that the mastery of four elements of Salt, Fat, Acid, and Heat are the keys to successful cuisine, these two songs pay homage to food

Name: Music
Description: Originating in the mid 1960s, with James Browns development of a signature groove that emphasized the downbeat, these two songs should be broadcast directly from the mothership through the station W-E-F-U-N-K directly into your eardrum with 500,000 kilowatts of power

Name: Colors
Description: Often lost in the shuffle, Sir Isaac Newton's treatise 'Optics' published in 1704, forever changed mankind's understanding of light and it's refraction properties, spawning countless children's songs and and Irish treasure hunts, his identification of ROYGBIV pales in comparison to inventing physics but serves as inspiration to these two tracks

Name: Before & After
Description: Paying homage to the most commonly repeated jeopardy category, the title of the second track begins with the final word of the first track
